LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEFENDANTS LAW OFFICE OF YUEN T. GIN, YUEN T. GIN, INC. AND YUEN GIN'S MOTION TO COMPEL PLAINTIFFS (1) FURTHER RESPONSES TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES, SETS ONE AND TWO AND REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS, SETS ONE AND TWO, AND (2) PRODUCTION OF RESPONSIVE.- PLAINTIFFS' FAILURE TO VERIFY THEIR RESPONSES TO DEFENDANTS' SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES (SET ONE) RENDERS THE CONCLUSIONS TO THEIR RESPONSES TO THE DISPUTED INTERROGATORIES (" . . . NOT APPLICABLE") INEFFECTIVE AND THAT PORTION OF THEIR RESPONSES WILL BE DISREGARDED. PLAINTIFFS' OBJECTIONS FOR LACK OF RELEVANCE AND INVASION OF PRIVACY ARE OVERRULED TO THE EXTENT THAT DEFENDANTS' SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES (SETS ONE AND TWO) AND REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS (SETS ONE AND TWO) SEEK INFORMATION CONCERNING THE RESOURCES OF BETTY WHELDEN. SAID OBJECTIONS ARE OTHERWISE SUSTAINED. PLAINTIFFS' OBJECTIONS FOR VAGUENESS, AMBIGUITY, OVER BREADTH, UNCERTAINTY, BURDENSOMENESS, OPPRESSION, ARGUMENTATIVENESS, ADOPTION OF ASSUMPTIONS, AND FORM ARE OVERRULED. PLAINTIFFS SHALL PROVIDE A VERIFIED FURTHER RESPONSE TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORY NO. 4 AND VERIFIED FURTHER RESPONSES AND RESPONSIVE DOCUMENTS TO REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION NOS. 2, 5-6 (BALANCES ONLY - TRANSACTION DATA REDACTED), AND 8 (CREDIT BALANCES ONLY - DEBIT BALANCES AND TRANSACTION DATA REDACTED) WITHIN 30 DAYS AFTER ENTRY OF AN APPROPRIATE PROTECTIVE ORDER. PLAINTIFF BLISS AS ATTORNEY-IN-FACT FOR WHELDEN SHALL PROVIDE VERIFIED FURTHER RESPONSES AND RESPONSIVE DOCUMENTS TO REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION NOS. 3, 4 AND 9 (AS TO FINANCIAL ASSETS ONLY) WITHIN 30 DAYS AFTER ENTRY OF AN APPROPRIATE PROTECTIVE ORDER. THE PARTIES SHALL MEET AND CONFER AS TO AN APPROPRIATE FORM OF PROTECTIVE ORDER. BOTH REQUESTS FOR SANCTIONS ARE DENIED. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER AND TO COMPLY WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; NOT REPORTED. =302/EG

Defendants Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiffs (1) Further Responses To Special Interrogatories, Sets One And Two And Requests For Production Of Documents, Sets One And Two, And (2) Production Of Responsive
Off Calendar Sep-25-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES - ATTY. JACK S. JOHAL, TEL. NO. 1-831-624-6473 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. CATHERINE M. LEE OF MUSICK PEELER, TEL. NO. 1-415-281-2024 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS.

RULING - A R G U E D; The court modified its tentative ruling and ruled as follows: DEFENDANTS LAW OFFICE OF YUEN T. GIN, YUEN T. GIN, INC. AND YUEN GIN'S Motion To Compel Plaintiffs (1) Further Responses To Special Interrogatories, Sets One And Two And Requests For Production Of Documents, Sets One And Two, And (2) Production Of Responsive.- Plaintiffs' failure to verify their responses to defendants' Special Interrogatories (Set One) renders the conclusions to their responses to the disputed interrogatories (" . . . not applicable") ineffective and that portion of their responses will be disregarded. Plaintiffs' objections for lack of relevance and invasion of privacy are overruled to the extent that defendants' Special Interrogatories (Sets One and Two) and Requests for Production of Documents (Sets One and Two) seek information concerning the resources of Betty Whelden. Said objections are otherwise sustained. Plaintiffs' objections for vagueness, ambiguity, over breadth, uncertainty, burdensomeness, oppression, argumentativeness, adoption of assumptions, and form are overruled. Plaintiffs shall provide a verified further response to Special Interrogatory No. 4 and verified further responses and responsive documents to Requests for Production Nos. 2, 5-6 (balances only - transaction data redacted), and 8 (credit balances only - debit balances and transaction data redacted) within 30 days after entry of an appropriate protective order. Plaintiff Bliss as attorney-in-fact for Whelden shall provide verified further responses and responsive documents to Requests for Production Nos. 3, 4 and 9 (as to financial assets only) within 30 days after entry of an appropriate protective order. The parties shall meet and confer as to an appropriate form of protective order. To the extent that plaintiffs contend that any Special Interrogatory or Request for Production is not applicable because the event or condition referred to therein did not occur or exist, plaintiffs' further responses shall state the basis of such inapplicability unequivocally and without ambiguous reference to their objections or understandings. To the extent that plaintiffs' responses are limited to time periods other than as stated in the Special Interrogatory or Request for Production, plaintiffs shall state the basis of such limitation unequivocally and without ambiguous reference to their objections or understandings. Both requests for sanctions are denied. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order and to comply with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Not reported. =302/eg
